MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Brian Geremania, Esq, for Joseph Dodson and Gary Dodson.
Steve Bovarnick, Esq., for JoAnna Teixeira.

The following related actions are simultaneously called: PES-24-307026 and PES-24-307306.

At the outset, the court acknowledges a stipulation to consolidate, submitted as an exhibit, and confirms with counsel that they stipulate to the consolidation of the related matters. The court orders case numbers PES-24-307026 and PES-24-307306 consolidated for all purposes, with all subsequent filings to be filed under the lead case, case number PES-24-307026, and the court endorses an order accordingly. There is discussion regarding service to Francisco Sr. and whether the parties wish for the court to issue a Breslin Order. Mr. Bovarnick offers an update regarding the service issue advising that the preceding was addressed in his response filed on Jan-10-2025. Counsel confirm that they are agreeable to the court issuing a Breslin Order. Mr. Bovarnick advises of his intent to serve the Breslin Order pursuant to the power of attorney for Francisco Sr. The court advises that it shall issue the Breslin Order which the court will serve on counsel of record and will include instructions re service to interested parties. The question of whether this matter might be set for trial is discussed. Mr. Geremania reports that he believes that a trial in this matter would take more than six (6) hours. The court advises that considering Mr. Geremania's report, this action would be sent to Department 206 for trial setting, if needed.

The subject matters are continued to Sep-16-2025 at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status Hearing RE Mediation and a Status of Trial Setting will be set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Thompson. Not Reported.
